title: Encyclia chloroleuca
intro: Encyclia chloroleuca, also known as the Panama yellow orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Encyclia chloroleuca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Encyclia chloroleuca is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Encyclia chloroleuca is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Within the family, it is placed in the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Cymbidieae. The genus Encyclia is further grouped under the subtribe Zeuxininae. This classification is based on taxonomic studies and is widely accepted in the botanical community.
uses: Encyclia chloroleuca is a species of orchid, but information about its medicinal uses is scarce or unavailable.
